flagged_setups.thesis: Post-earnings momentum setup: $1.63B revenue +85% YoY, EPS $0.33 +18% beat, FY guide raised 10% to $7.65B. US Commercial +133% to $595M. Muted AH reaction (+0.84%) on valuation (~200x fwd). Watch open print at 16:30 IST — if stock holds above $147 and builds, momentum continuation into 5-day window. Key risk: Q4 repeat (stock dropped 11.6% day after prior quarter on valuation). Rule of 40 at 145% is extraordinary.
flagged_setups.price_note: $147.26 AH close May 4 (regular close $146.03) · CNBC / TradingView May 4 2026

flagged_setups.thesis: Post-earnings setup pending tonight's Q1 AH report. Est: Rev $9.9B (+33%), EPS $1.29, Data Center $5.56B (+51.5%), non-GAAP GM ~55%. Three-condition entry: clean DC beat + credible MI325 roadmap + margin holds. Stock moved $341.54 (Mon close, risk-off Hormuz) to ~$360 pre-market — all of that pre-earnings pricing. If beat+raise confirmed, enter on May 6 open. Binary event — miss on margin = sharp reversal risk.
flagged_setups.price_note: ~$360 pre-market May 5 (Mon close $341.54) · Investing.com / Capital.com May 5 2026

flagged_setups.thesis: Structural supply shock deepened: Iran attacks Fujairah oil hub (Hormuz bypass endpoint), breaking ceasefire. Brent peaked $114.44 (4-year high) and now holds $113.54. With both Hormuz AND Fujairah bypass compromised, structural supply disruption is more severe than prior week. No diplomatic breakthrough in sight. IEA cited 'largest supply disruption in history.' Support floor above $110 until Fujairah repair and/or ceasefire re-established. Sharp reversal risk on any peace breakthrough.
flagged_setups.price_note: $113.54 Brent at 02:00 GMT May 5 (peak $114.44 Mon close) · CNBC / Al Jazeera May 5 2026
